title: OSMM Content Strategy Object
description: The content plan for an offering or campaign: the content goal, the content pillars/themes (and the formats under each), the content priorities and types in scope, how content is sequenced across the journey/funnel, and the publishing cadence. Canonical schema for object_type 'content_strategy'. The osmm-content-strategy-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape. Content Strategy is the *what content we produce and in what order* (5.3) — distinct from Creative Strategy (the creative direction / how it looks and feels), the Messaging Framework (the message architecture it conveys), and the Journey (the path it supports).

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

content_strategy_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'CTS-' + lowercase hyphen slug. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: A readable label for the content strategy, usually entity + offering (e.g. 'IBM watsonx Content Strategy').

content_goal.description: The outcome the content plan drives — what producing this content is meant to achieve (an outcome, not a list of deliverables).

content_pillars.description: The content themes/pillars and the formats produced under each — the spine of the plan (5.3).

pillar.description: The pillar's name — a recurring content theme the plan organizes around.

description.description: What this pillar covers and why it advances the content goal.

formats.description: The content formats/types produced under this pillar (e.g. blog, video, webinar). Optional.

content_priorities.description: What to produce first / where to concentrate effort — the emphasis order across pillars or topics. Optional.

content_types.description: The formats/types in scope overall (e.g. blog, video, webinar, social, email, case study, whitepaper). Optional.

sequencing.description: How content is sequenced across the journey/funnel — ordering and pacing of content by stage (5.3). Optional.

cadence.description: Publishing frequency/pacing (e.g. 'weekly long-form, daily social'). Optional.

channels.description: Channels the content is published to/through. Optional.

journey_mapping.description: Which content supports which journey stage — the funnel/journey-to-content mapping (5.3). Optional.

content.description: The content produced for / serving this stage.

linked_messaging_framework.description: Id of the Messaging Framework whose message architecture this content conveys. 'MSF-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_creative_strategy.description: Id of the Creative Strategy that sets the creative direction this content is produced within. 'CRS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_keywords.description: Ids of the target Keywords/topics this content addresses. 'KW-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_personas.description: Ids of the Persona objects this content is written for. 'PER-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_journey.description: Id of the Journey this content supports — the path the sequencing maps against. 'JNY-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_campaign_strategy.description: Id of the Campaign Strategy if this content strategy is scoped to a specific campaign. 'CMS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

linked_business_context.description: Id of the owning Business Context. 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' ok. Optional.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date. Optional.
